content:
Bring your Next.js interfaces to life using nothing but modern CSS. Create smooth, performant animations that feel polished without adding a single line of JavaScript.

4 min read
1 hour ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

How to Use CSS Animations in Next.js Without JavaScriptAnimations have a funny reputation in frontend development. When they are done well, nobody notices them. The interface simply feels responsive and enjoyable to use. When they are done poorly, every interaction suddenly feels slower than it actually is.
Early in my career, I reached for JavaScript whenever something needed to move. A dropdown? JavaScript. A fading notification? JavaScript. A loading effect? More JavaScript. It worked, but it also meant more code to maintain and more opportunities for layout thrashing or unnecessary re renders.
Modern CSS has come a long way. Combined with the App Router in Next.js, you can build surprisingly rich interactions while keeping your application lightweight and fast.
Why prefer CSS animations?
For many UI interactions, CSS is simply the better tool.
It allows the browser to optimize animations efficiently, often using the GPU for properties like transform and opacity. That means smoother…
